BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the landlord mentioned property taxes might bump the Atwells Avenue lease. Pre-Russell & Associates review.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Retail counter is the base, wholesale accounts run about $6,200/mo, and custom and wedding cakes carry the rest. Food cost running 31% vs 28% target with King Arthur flour up 15%. Revisit with Russell & Associates once 2025 actuals close.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $4,200/mo if the property-tax reassessment passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $22,500 + $400 rent increase = $22,900. At a 69% contribution margin after 31% food cost, $22,900 / 0.69 = $33,188 in monthly sales to break even.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. We clear break-even by roughly $6.8K a month at current $40K sales.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $4,500/mo (unlikely per Ferrara Properties)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Ferrara Properties said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is roughly $33K in monthly sales at the current cost structure - fixed costs around $22.5K against a 69% margin after food cost. At $40K in sales we have solid headroom. Main concern is the summer wedding-cake season swing and the winter retail dip - even a slow February clears break-even.
ActionItems: Confirm the property-tax pass-through with the landlord before renewing the lease
Revisit after year-end with Russell & Associates for proper modeling
Re-price a few retail items if food cost stays above 31%
